title: potentilla litoralis
intro: Potentilla litoralis, also known as the littoral cinquefoil, is a perennial plant species native to the coastal areas of Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and five-petaled leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, skin conditions, and infections. Potentilla litoralis has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and pain, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed internally to harness its medicinal properties.
